summary: Body copy min 16px font, line height ≥150% (increase for light-on-dark), apply type scale for hierarchy, ensure all text is legible.
Ensure sufficient contrast and do not use color alone to convey information for accessibility compliance.
Forms require persistent visible labels (no placeholders or disappearing labels) and clickable/tappable targets must be at least 48px in size.
Use quick, non-disruptive transitions; avoid excessive motion; allow pausing; design responsively for phone, tablet, desktop; no autoplay motion over ~5s without static fallback.
[GENERAL email] Animated GIFs must display the key message in frame 1 to ensure visibility in Outlook, which shows only the first frame.
All images require descriptive alt text; transcripts mandatory for all video/audio content.
Follow WCAG (wcag.com) as the reference accessibility standard for all materials.
Use "someone/people with dermatomyositis" for patient (DTC) materials; "patients" OK in HCP; always confirm audience in briefs before writing.
The patient brochure is the master reference for visuals, messaging, and tone across all LISRAYA materials.
Each 600px-wide <table> section has top/bottom 32px padding, distinct background color, no outside margins; sections stack directly without gaps.
Each section's metadata must specify background color, dermatomyositis (DM) status, assigned footnote symbols (†, ‡) avoiding collisions, and CTA presence.
Footnote symbols are superscript, assigned in appearance order at assembly; multiple referenced claims require section placeholders (e.g., {{fn1}}), no hard-coded daggers.
Locked components (header, footer, ISI) inserted by core team; adjacent sections must avoid decorative elements visually dependent on neighboring locked components.
Assembly rule: Avoid placing dark-background sections adjacent; assembler ensures this using declared section backgrounds.
Use HEX colors, px sizing, inline CSS only; duplicate bgcolor attributes for Outlook in all code for email-client compatibility.

LISRAYA palette uses primary (Brand Blue #00529b, Sunshine #faa31b, Gold #ffc60a), secondary (Sky Blue #358CCB, Deep Blue #003D74, Dark Navy #011E45), tertiary (Coral #F26A38, Light Blue #E6F0F9, Golden Sand #FEF1C8, Graphite #212121, White #ffffff) tiers with fixed dominance: primary > secondary > tertiary.
Apply color palette in primary > secondary > tertiary ratio with blues and golds dominant; tertiary colors serve as accents and surfaces.
Headlines use Brand Blue or White on dark; body text Graphite #212121 on light, White on dark with line height ↑; links Brand Blue #00529b underlined on light, White underlined on dark.
Use Brand Blue + Sky Blue as dominant chart colors; Sunshine/Gold only for key figure emphasis; gray for placebo per chart.color_logic, chart.emphasis.color, chart.placebo.color.
Callout surfaces: Light Blue 70% tint for data; Golden Sand 30% tint for text-heavy callouts.
Use Coral sparingly as a tertiary accent; never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data; avoid in email sections unless specified by the brief.
Logo icon uses 70% transparent Pantone 7548 C (#ffc60a) in all instances, including marks and patterns.
Only use palette colors or specified tints: campaign headline D/M at ~40% tint (Gold/Brand Blue), Light Blue callout 70%, Golden Sand callout 30%, wave top layer 70% opacity.
Use ≈40% tint on campaign headline 'D'/'M' (Gold/Brand Blue), 70% tint Light Blue callout, 30% tint Golden Sand callout, 70% transparent wave tops.

Use only approved gradients with exact stops for backgrounds: Brand Blue → Dark Navy (#00529b→#011E45) dark; Sky Blue → Deep Blue (#358CCB→#003D74) blue; Sunshine → 75% Gold (#faa31b→Gold75%) warm; Golden Sand → White → Light Blue (#FEF1C8→#ffffff→#E6F0F9) soft light; Golden Sand → 75% Gold → Sunshine (#FEF1C8→Gold75%→#faa31b) warm light-to-saturated.
Masthead gradient Sunshine to light gold (#ffe05c) used with logo mark, logo placed at the gradient's lightest end for contrast.
In masthead/banner, apply horizontal gradient from dark/saturated left to light right.
Use approved gradients as backgrounds, with wave graphic, and on LISRAYA logo mark only.
Test text/background contrast with WCAG AA minimums: 4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text ≥24px or 19px bold using webaim.com or equivalent.
Use Brand Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy, Graphite text on White/Light Blue/Golden Sand; White text on Brand Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy; avoid Gold/Sunshine text on White.
Do not use color alone for meaning; include icons, depth, text, or visual feedback for accessibility.

Gradient Callout CTA: full-width 600px content banner with orange/yellow gradient bg cover no-repeat centered; headline top third Brand Blue #00529b Nunito Sans 800 ~28px; button below center Deep Blue #003D74 fill, white Nunito Sans 800 18px label, Gold #FFC20E 24px arrow, 248×64px.

Gradient Callout background (Gradient_callout.png) must not be re-hosted/recolored; always fallback to solid Sunshine #faa31b background-color; background image skipped only in rare cases, retaining #faa31b color and all other style rules.
Gradient Callout button must use four-value asymmetric border-radius (49.52px 1.76px 40px 1.76px) exactly, no simplification, swapping, or rounding.

Icon Callout Box: Light Blue #E6F0F9 box on White #ffffff (16px 25px padding), Brand Blue #00529b Nunito Sans 800 ~21px/28px left, vertically center; White 80px rounded badge with 48px Brand Blue stroke icon half-overlay left edge; copy avoids badge overlap; for proof points.
Geometry: badge width W=80px default; inset blue box left by W/2=40px; badge center aligns on box left edge; box padding-left=W/2+24=64px; badge margin-top= -H/2 for vertical center.
Corner radius fixed: box 40px 8px 40px 8px (TL TR BR BL), badge 20px 4px 20px 4px; never uniform, swapped, or flipped diagonally.
White icon badge requires subtle drop shadow (0 4px 10px rgba(1,30,69,0.18)) on all instances to lift it off blue box.

Use absolute-positioned 50/50 badge overlay for most clients; pre-composite as transparent PNG or background image for pixel-perfect Outlook, else accept badge flush left with unchanged colors and radii.

Email body fixed at 600px centered; sections edge-to-edge 600px; mobile breakpoint 480px with all multi-columns collapsing to single column; internal grid uses 12-column model with 1-col, 2-col 50/50, or image/text ≈40/60.

Section padding 24px left/right always; full-bleed backgrounds max 600px but text respects 24px insets; vertical rhythm 8px base unit; 32px top/bottom padding per section with zero-gap stacking; gaps: heading→body 16px, paragraph→paragraph 16px, body→callout 24px, image→caption 8px.
Primary CTA: #00529b bg, #fff text, Nunito Sans Bold 16px, 14px 32px padding, min 48px height, 24px pill radius; Secondary CTA: #fff bg, 2px #00529b border, #00529b text same metrics.
One primary CTA allowed per section maximum.
Brand waves use only Sunshine/Gold or Sky Blue/Deep Blue colorways, both in long/short variants; reference agr_lisraya_waves assets.
Waves use a top wave layer at 70% transparency over base (Gold over Sunshine; Sky Blue over Deep Blue), preserving layered translucency.
Waves used alone or over photos/gradients to support layout flow, frame content, or add depth.
Use masthead gradient from Sunshine to light gold (#ffe05c) with logo mark at lightest end for masthead copy per tok_lisraya_masthead_gradient and ast_lisraya_masthead_logo_mark.
Imagery: Use warm, realistic patient lifestyle photos of simple, everyday activities (walking dog, beach, BBQ) showing returning to normal life.
Imagery uses Accent shape (§2.5) with asymmetric rounded corners, no plain rectangles/circles; waves and gradients may overlay photos.
Every image must include descriptive alt text to meet brand accessibility standards.
Brand Accent Shape is a rectangle with opposite corner radii: large 1.25"/0.5"/0.4375" plus 0.0625" (print) or 120/48/42px plus 6px (email), large radius on one diagonal pair.
Use consistent diagonal accent-shape radius orientation in emails; default large radius on top-left and bottom-right across all sections.
Charts and tables use uniform 12px rounded corners on all corners, not the asymmetric accent shape.
Chart containers use Light Blue panels with uniform rounded corners and Brand Blue rounded header bars for clean, scan-friendly, consistent design.
Chart colors: Brand Blue, Sky Blue dominant; Sunshine, Gold for key data emphasis; placebo/comparator is neutral gray; set in chart.dominant.color, chart.emphasis.color, chart.placebo.color.
Imagery rule: Bars and chart icons require rounded, approachable shapes like rounded bar tops.
Chart headlines: Agenda (print) or Nunito Sans Bold+ (email), ALL CAPS, Brand Blue text, thin Gold rule beneath.
Statistical callouts: large bold % figure as hero, lighter supporting copy below; footnotes smaller and clearly separated from primary data.
Iconography uses minimal line illustration with consistent stroke weight, rounded caps, single-color lines; new icons must match this style.
Icon strokes default to Brand Blue on light backgrounds; icon colors and backgrounds adjustable only within the brand palette (iconography.color).
iconography general: minimum 32×32px size in email to ensure scale and contrast for low-vision audiences (iconography.min_size)
Maintain generous white space; keep sections clean and uncluttered; limit focal points per section; use contrast to highlight one primary element.
Use imagery and icons to break up text walls, following the "Show, don't tell" layout rule.
Alternate White, Light Blue, Golden Sand, gradient backgrounds for My Compass sections; section metadata must declare background color to avoid adjacent duplicate colors.
Use bold headline, simple benefit statements, and patient quote as canonical content block pattern per patient brochure for all LISRAYA materials.
Always write LISRAYA in ALL CAPS in all written materials, superseding Feb 2026 guide's title-case rule.
Apply ® only with generic name on first prominent mention in external-facing docs; do not repeat on subsequent mentions (e.g., LISRAYA® (brepocitinib)).
Generic name format: lowercase, in parentheses, e.g., (brepocitinib).
Parent company sign-off "Priovant Therapeutics, Inc." only in locked footer; exclude from all content sections.

Headlines: Agenda (print), Nunito Sans bold Title Case (digital/email), Aptos fallback; Body/footer: Nunito Sans (Aptos fallback); Agenda weights Light-Bold; Nunito Sans Light-ExtraBold; Aptos Regular/Bold; Agenda live text only in digital campaign headline.
Email font stack: 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; load Nunito Sans via @font-face/Google Fonts if supported for graceful fallback in Outlook and others.

Email typography: H1 28px/36px Nunito Sans ExtraBold/Bold; H2 22px/30px Bold; H3 18px/26px Bold/SemiBold; Body 16px/24px Regular (min); Secondary 14px/21px sparing; Footnote 12px/18px small & separate.
Line height minimum 150% for all body copy; use 160% for light text on dark backgrounds (e.g., 16px/26px) per brand accessibility.
Use distinct type scale and hierarchy; never use adjacent text blocks with same size and weight for different hierarchy levels.
Headlines use title case: capitalize nouns, pronouns, adjectives, verbs, adverbs; lowercase articles, conjunctions, prepositions; example: 'LISRAYA Targets the Root Cause'.
Campaign headline 'YOU DESERVE MORE' always in ALL CAPS, bound to campaign_headline.case (case.campaign_headline.all_caps).
Chart/infographic headlines use ALL CAPS in Agenda (print) or Nunito Sans Bold (email fallback), tied to chart.headline.case and chart.headline.font.
Body copy uses sentence case, linked to body.case (case.body.sentence_case).
Avoid ALL CAPS in body copy, buttons, subheads except campaign headline 'YOU DESERVE MORE', chart/infographic headlines, and brand name only.

Use bold for hierarchy/key takeaways only: max headings, one key phrase/claim per paragraph, callout text/numbers (§1.7), first brand name in benefits; avoid full paragraphs, footnotes, >15% text, prepositions, generic names.
Never combine bold, ALL CAPS, and color emphasis simultaneously on one string; use max two emphasis types, except chart headlines and campaign headline lockup.
No underlining except hyperlinks; italics only for study names, notes ('Note:'), and citations; no italics for emphasis in patient copy.
Use "dermatomyositis (DM)" at first mention, then "DM" in running copy; never use "DM" in headlines, headings, or subheads.
[GENERAL] Each standalone section must introduce 'dermatomyositis (DM)' upon first mention unless assembly brief confirms prior abbreviation use.
In patient materials, use "someone/people with dermatomyositis," not "patients"; use "care team" instead of "staff"; "patients" allowed only in HCP materials.
Spell out 0–9, use numerals 10+; always numerals for percentages (%), ages, ratios; e.g., 74%
Use empathetic, supportive tone citing clinical data; avoid negative/fear language; maintain trust without oversimplification (e.g., VALOR study results).
Use Oxford/serial comma in series of three or more items.
'YOU DESERVE MORE' headline uses Agenda (email fallback Nunito Sans ExtraBold), all caps, Gold/Sunshine or Brand Blue color, 'D' and 'M' at 40% tint, hero stacks 3 lines left-aligned.
Email campaign headline uses Nunito Sans ExtraBold fallback; Agenda reserved as live text only on digital surfaces for this headline.
Full campaign lockup copy: 'YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.' verbatim.
Use three callout styles (main, secondary/data, text-heavy); never place two identical callouts competing on one screen or section.
Main callout: bold Brand Blue text, centered, with 2px Gold horizontal rules above/below, 60–100% text block width, 16px padding between rule and text.
Secondary/data callout: Light Blue box 70% tint, bold Brand Blue text, optional 1 line icon, 20px padding, corner radius per §2.5 accent-shape rules.
Text-heavy callout uses 30% Golden Sand box, bold Brand Blue headline, line icons, bold Sky Blue subheads, Graphite body, 24px padding, two-column collapsing on mobile.
Use the exact campaign headline lockup: "YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.

Use exact product benefits phrase "LISRAYA is a once-daily FDA-approved pill that blocks specific inflammatory signals driving dermatomyositis, so you can:" then bullet points: Increase your muscle strength, Improve your skin symptoms, Rely less on steroids, Do more of the activities you love.
Use mechanism-of-action copy verbatim: Primary "targets root cause of DM," alternate "turns off specific inflammatory switches stuck 'on'.
Admin claim exact phrase: "LISRAYA is a once-daily pill—you can take it at home or anywhere you like, with no shots or infusions required.
Doctor-discussion CTA must exactly read: "Talk to your doctor about LISRAYA, the DM treatment you may be looking for.
Patient support program name "My Compass Support" must be referenced verbatim, offering Personalized Communication via PAL (text, phone, email, in person) and Authorization Support.
LISRAYA voice: Confident (clear, direct, not boastful), warm (genuine empathy, patient-focused), clear (plain, respectful, accurate); tone flexes slightly, core voice constant.
Clinical claims require † markers; footnotes must be clearly separated and set smaller per §1.2 footnote type scale.
Patient quotes use `- [Name], LISRAYA patient` format, italic/distinguished style, paired with warm lifestyle imagery per patient_story.quote.style token.
Priovant logo, copyright, ® legal, job code [mm/yy], Indication, ISI reside only in locked header/footer, never in content sections.
Use brand colors in order: Deep Blue #00529B dominant, Amber Orange #FAA31B secondary accent, Golden Yellow #FFC60A tertiary accent.
Agenda font designated for all primary headlines and headers (headline.font).
Use Nunito Sans for all print body copy (body.font); load on web via Google Fonts CSS2 import URL with ital, opsz, wght axes.
Use internal banner CTA as 180×59px inline SVG button with asymmetric rounded corners filled by #FFDF55 to #FAA21B gradient.
